title: Four-shaft rosepath weave
subtitle: Threading and treadling a classic Scandinavian rosepath pattern on a four-shaft loom
excerpt: Rosepath is a traditional Scandinavian weave structure that produces a characteristic star or rose motif from a simple four-shaft threading and six-treadle sequence. This entry covers the threading, tie-up, and pattern treadling for a rosepath sampler.
sourceNotes: Rosepath threading documented in Scandinavian weaving tradition, referenced in historical textile catalogues and weaving manuals. The structure appears in Swedish and Norwegian traditional weaving from the 17th century onward.

glossaryTerms.definition: A traditional Scandinavian four-shaft threading that produces a star or rose motif when woven with a specific treadling. The threading is a point threading variant: 1-2-3-4-3-2-3-4-3-2-1.
A plain-weave pass inserted between each pattern pick in a rosepath sequence. The tabby pass locks the pattern floats in place and prevents the cloth from being too open or unstable.
A weft or warp thread that passes over two or more opposing threads without interlacing. In rosepath, controlled floats create the petal and star shapes.

content.text: threading to produce its star motif. Each pattern pick needs a
in place. Without the tabby, the floats lie unsupported and the cloth is unstable. The pattern-and-tabby alternation produces the spotted-rose look rather than a solid block.

items.name: Smooth cotton or wool warp in a plain ground colour
Contrasting pattern weft (slightly thicker than the warp)
Tabby weft matching the warp colour

content.text: Thread the rosepath repeat: 1-2-3-4-3-2-3-4-3-2-1. This 10-thread unit produces one complete rose motif. Repeat as many times as the warp width allows. Note that shafts 1 and 2 carry fewer heddles than shafts 3 and 4 in this threading; rebalance heddles before threading if needed.

Treadle the rosepath sequence from the draft, alternating each pattern pick with a tabby pick. For a standard rosepath flower: one pattern pick, one tabby. Repeat for each of the six pattern picks per motif repeat. The motifs stack vertically as you weave; each full treadling sequence produces one rose motif height.

items.problem: The rose motifs look blocky or the floats are too long
items.cause: The tabby picks were omitted between pattern picks
items.fix: Rosepath requires the tabby pick between every pattern pick. Without the tabby, the floats become unsupported and the cloth is unstable. Re-weave the motif with alternating tabby passes.

items.problem: The rosepath motif does not look like a rose; it looks like vertical stripes
items.cause: A threading error in the 3-4-3-2 section of the repeat, likely a shaft-3 and shaft-2 swap
items.fix: Re-check the threading in the middle of each motif: shafts 3 and 4 should alternate four times before reaching the mirrored section. The characteristic rose shape comes from the specific threading sequence.
